_meta.title: Philip Fisher Scalability Codex — Technical Specification Ledger
_meta.author: Philip A. Fisher (Common Stocks and Uncommon Profits)
_meta.purpose: Authoritative source of truth for all vectorized operating leverage metrics, pricing power acceleration thresholds, and capital dilution guards inside Project Beast.
_meta.threshold_provenance_note: CRITICAL: Fisher's original book (PDF, 353 pages) contains ZERO numerical thresholds. All percentage values in this spec are India-adapted engineering proxies derived from the companion handbook and system engineering judgment — NOT Fisher's own formulas. Fisher uses qualitative language: 'worthwhile profit margin', 'largely cancels shareholder benefit', 'truly outstanding'. No future developer should claim 'Fisher says X%' for any threshold in this file.
_meta.pdf_audit_verdict: All 4 Fisher Scalability gates confirmed faithful to Fisher's directional intent. No hidden quantitative parameters exist in the original text. Framework is correctly implemented.

unit_notes.d05_rev_minus_exp_gr: PERCENTAGE DELTA — 2.0 = revenue growing 2pp faster than expenses
unit_notes.opm_acceleration: MARGIN POINTS — 0.0 threshold = OPM not declining vs prior year
unit_notes.npm_acceleration: MARGIN POINTS — 0.0 threshold = NPM not declining vs prior year
unit_notes.dilution_pct: PERCENTAGE — 1.0 = 1% annual share count growth ceiling

revenue_runway_scalability._note: Points 1 & 2: Verifies a continuous multi-year demand runway for the company's product line. 3Y CAGR ensures sustained trajectory; YoY gate confirms no recent deceleration.

operating_leverage_inflection._note: Points 5 & 6: Requires revenue expansion to scale faster than internal operational expense growth. d05_rev_minus_exp_gr = rev_gr_yoy - d04_expense_gr_yoy. Threshold 2pp ensures signal is not measurement noise.

pricing_power_moat.logic: OR (either OPM or NPM must be non-declining)
pricing_power_moat._note: Point 4: OR logic used because manufacturing/asset-heavy businesses compress OPM via depreciation while NPM stays flat; OR prevents false rejects. Threshold >= 0 = margins at minimum flat vs prior year (not declining).

capital_dilution_shield.fillna_strategy: fillna(999) — missing share count data treated as worst case (excluded)
capital_dilution_shield._note: Point 13: Clamps down on share printing, restricting capital access exclusively to self-funding enterprises. fillna(999) is conservative — cannot verify zero dilution if data is missing.

implementation_mapping._score_range: 0-4 (one point per sub-gate: rev_runway + op_lev + pricing + anti_dilut)

scoring_matrix.description: fisher_score is 0-4 (sum of 4 boolean sub-gates). fisher_pass = 1 only when all 4 pass (AND logic). The score enables partial-pass ranking: a company with 3/4 gates is closer than one with 1/4.

fw_fisher_quality.focus: Existing Fisher Quality proxy — forensic integrity + margin quality + zero dilution
fw_fisher_quality.label: Fisher Quality

fw_fisher_scalability.focus: Operating leverage inflection + scalability runway + capital discipline — the 4 most predictive quantifiable Fisher criteria
fw_fisher_scalability.label: Fisher Scalability

vectorization_matrix.description: All 4 gates are pure vectorized Pandas/NumPy boolean masks. Zero loops, zero apply(), zero iterrows(). NaN strategy is conservative: missing data defaults to gate failure (dilution fillna(999)) or neutral-pass (revenue/oplev fillna(0) which may pass or fail depending on threshold).

nan_handling.rev_gr_3y: fillna(0) — missing 3Y revenue data → 0 < 12 → gate fails (conservative)
nan_handling.rev_gr_yoy: fillna(0) — missing YoY data → 0 < 10 → gate fails
nan_handling.opm_acceleration: fillna(0) — missing OPM accel → 0 >= 0 → OR gate passes (neutral; npm guards this)
nan_handling.npm_acceleration: fillna(0) — missing NPM accel → 0 >= 0 → OR gate passes (neutral; opm guards this)
nan_handling.dilution_pct: fillna(999) — CONSERVATIVE: missing share data → 999 > 1 → gate fails (cannot confirm no dilution)

fisher_pass.description: Binary pass flag for Fisher Scalability (Framework 33). 1 = all 4 scalability gates cleared. 0 = one or more gates failed.
fisher_pass.filters_to: Stocks currently in an operating leverage inflection window

fisher_score.description: Count of Fisher Scalability sub-gates passed. Enables partial-pass ranking. Score 4 = all gates clear (identical to fisher_pass=1). Score 3 = one gate blocking.
fisher_score.use_case: Sort by fisher_score descending to rank near-miss scalability candidates

fisher_quality_pass.description: Binary pass flag for Fisher Quality (Framework 11). 1 = all 7 quality gates cleared (forensic integrity + margins + cash quality + zero dilution). Mirrors the pattern of ub_pass, baid_pass, diamonds_pass for cross-strategy filtering without string parsing.
fisher_quality_pass.filters_to: Stocks that are structurally elite Fisher-quality businesses

quadrant_logic.⚪ Laggard: fisher_quality_pass=0 AND fisher_pass=0 — fails both quality and scalability
quadrant_logic.⚡ Catalyst Play: fisher_quality_pass=0 AND fisher_pass=1 — inflection firing but structural quality absent; trading candidate only
quadrant_logic.🐢 Steady Compounder: fisher_quality_pass=1 AND fisher_pass=0 — elite quality proven, no current inflection; steady-state long hold
quadrant_logic.👑 Apex Winner: fisher_quality_pass=1 AND fisher_pass=1 — elite quality business AT its operating leverage peak; prime entry signal

strategic_notes.Apex Winner frequency: Rarest quadrant — requires simultaneous structural quality AND timing inflection; typically <5% of universe
strategic_notes.Steady Compounder frequency: Most common passing quadrant — quality businesses not currently at inflection
strategic_notes.Catalyst Play risk: Structurally weak but inflecting — high-alpha trade candidate, not a compounder; position sizing discipline required
